content.text:
Perfect! I have successfully read all the requested files. Here is a comprehensive report of the existing channel plugin patterns:

---

## IRC CHANNEL PLUGIN

### 1. `/extensions/irc/package.json`
Standard plugin package with metadata:
- Name: `@openclaw/irc` v2026.4.1-beta.1
- Type: module
- Extensions entry: `./index.ts`
- Setup entry: `./setup-entry.ts`
- Channel metadata: id="irc", label="IRC", docs at "/channels/irc"
- Aliases: ["internet-relay-chat"]
- System image: "network"
- Min host version: >=2026.4.1

### 2. `/extensions/irc/openclaw.plugin.json`
- id: "irc"
- channels: ["irc"]
- configSchema: empty object (no plugin-level config)

### 3. `/extensions/irc/index.ts`
Entry point that:
- Imports `ChannelPlugin` type and `defineChannelPluginEntry` from SDK
- Imports plugin from `./src/channel.js`
- Imports runtime setup from `./src/runtime.js`
- Exports both
- Calls `defineChannelPluginEntry()` with:
  - id: "irc"
  - name: "IRC"
  - description: "IRC channel plugin"
  - plugin: ircPlugin (typed as ChannelPlugin)
  - setRuntime: setIrcRuntime

### 4. `/extensions/irc/setup-entry.ts`
- Calls `defineSetupPluginEntry(ircPlugin)` - passes plugin directly

### 5. `/extensions/irc/src/types.ts`
Defines configuration types:
- `IrcChannelConfig`: Per-channel settings (requireMention, tools, skills, systemPrompt, allowFrom, etc.)
- `IrcNickServConfig`: NickServ authentication (enabled, service, password, register, registerEmail)
- `IrcAccountConfig`: Full account config (name, enabled, host, port, tls, nick, username, realname, password, passwordFile, nickserv, dmPolicy, allowFrom, defaultTo, groupPolicy, groups, channels, mentionPatterns, markdown, historyLimit, dmHistoryLimit, dms, textChunkLimit, chunkMode, blockStreaming, blockStreamingCoalesce, responsePrefix, mediaMaxMb)
- `IrcConfig`: Allows multiple accounts + defaultAccount
- `CoreConfig`: OpenClawConfig with irc section
- `IrcInboundMessage`: message structure with messageId, target, senderNick, senderUser, senderHost, text, timestamp, isGroup
- `IrcProbe`: probe result extending BaseProbeResult with host, port, tls, nick, latencyMs

### 6. `/extensions/irc/src/runtime.ts`
- Creates plugin runtime store using `createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>`
- Exports `setIrcRuntime`, `getIrcRuntime`, `clearIrcRuntime`
- Error message: "IRC runtime not initialized"

### 7. `/extensions/irc/src/channel.ts`
Large plugin implementation (~357 lines) containing:

**Key patterns:**
- Creates `ircPlugin` using `createChatChannelPlugin()` with extensive configuration
- **base section**: 
  - id, meta, setup, setupWizard, capabilities (direct, group, media, blockStreaming)
  - reload config prefixes
  - configSchema reference
  - config adapter (scoped account accessor, allowFrom formatting, defaultTo resolution)
  - groups adapter (requireMention, toolPolicy resolution)
  - messaging adapter (normalizeTarget, targetResolver with looksLikeId hint)
  - resolver (resolveTargets async)
  - directory (listPeers, listGroups)
  - status (createComputedAccountStatusAdapter with probeAccount, buildChannelSummary)
  - gateway (startAccount with monitorIrcProvider, statusSink)

- **pairing section**: text-based with idLabel, message, normalizeAllowEntry, notify function
- **security section**: resolveDmPolicy, collectWarnings
- **outbound section**: deliveryMode "direct", chunker (markdown mode, 350 char limit), attachedResults (channel: "irc", sendText, sendMedia)

---

## NOSTR CHANNEL PLUGIN

### 8. `/extensions/nostr/package.json`
- Name: `@openclaw/nostr` v2026.4.1-beta.1
- Dependencies: `nostr-tools`: ^2.23.3
- Extensions: `./index.ts`
- Setup entry: `./setup-entry.ts`
- Channel metadata: id="nostr", label="Nostr", selectionLabel="Nostr (NIP-04 DMs)"
- Docs path: "/channels/nostr"
- Blurb: "Decentralized protocol; encrypted DMs via NIP-04"
- order: 55, quickstartAllowFrom: true
- Install: npmSpec: "@openclaw/nostr", minHostVersion: >=2026.4.1

### 9. `/extensions/nostr/openclaw.plugin.json`
- id: "nostr"
- channels: ["nostr"]
- configSchema: empty object

### 10. `/extensions/nostr/index.ts`
Entry point that:
- Imports `defineChannelPluginEntry` from SDK
- Imports plugin from `./src/channel.js`
- Imports types and HTTP handler
- Imports runtime getters/setters
- Exports plugin and setNostrRuntime
- Calls `defineChannelPluginEntry()` with id, name, description, plugin, setRuntime
- Has `registerFull()` method that registers HTTP route for `/api/channels/nostr` with auth="gateway"
- HTTP handler manages profile data with getConfigProfile, updateConfigProfile, getAccountInfo methods

### 11. `/extensions/nostr/setup-entry.ts`
- Calls `defineSetupPluginEntry(nostrPlugin)`

### 12. `/extensions/nostr/src/runtime.ts`
- Creates plugin runtime store using `createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>`
- Exports `setNostrRuntime`, `getNostrRuntime`
- Error message: "Nostr runtime not initialized"

### 13. `/extensions/nostr/src/channel.ts`
Large plugin implementation (~503 lines) containing:

**Key patterns:**
- Global state: activeBuses Map (per account), metricsSnapshots Map
- Helper functions: normalizeNostrAllowEntry, isNostrSenderAllowed, resolveNostrDirectAccess
- Scoped config adapters for Nostr
- Creates `nostrPlugin` using `createChatChannelPlugin()`:

- **base section**:
  - id, meta (no quickstart), capabilities (direct only, no media for MVP)
  - reload config prefixes
  - configSchema reference
  - config adapter (topLevel variant)
  - messaging (normalizeTarget with Nostr prefix stripping, targetResolver with npub/hex hint, resolveOutboundSessionRoute)
  - status (createComputedAccountStatusAdapter with buildPassiveChannelStatusSummary, collectStatusIssuesFromLastError)
  - gateway (startAccount):
    - Creates channel pairing controller
    - Starts NostrBus with comprehensive callbacks
    - Handles inbound messages with resolveNostrDirectAccess
    - Dispatches inbound via dispatchInboundDirectDmWithRuntime
    - Tracks metrics, relay connections, errors
    - Returns cleanup function

- **pairing section**: text-based with idLabel "nostrPubkey", message, normalizeAllowEntry, notify (sends via bus)

- **security section**: resolveDmPolicy with default "pairing"

- **outbound section**:
  - deliveryMode: "direct"
  - textChunkLimit: 4000
  - sendText: async function that gets bus, sends via bus.sendDm, returns attachChannelToResult

- **Exports**: getNostrMetrics(), getActiveNostrBuses(), publishNostrProfile(), getNostrProfileState()

---

## SDK TYPES

### 14. `/src/channels/plugins/types.plugin.ts` (first 200 lines)
Defines `ChannelPlugin<ResolvedAccount = any, Probe = unknown, Audit = unknown>` type with these sections:

```
- id: ChannelId
- meta: ChannelMeta
- capabilities: ChannelCapabilities
- defaults?: { queue?: { debounceMs? } }
- reload?: { configPrefixes: string[]; noopPrefixes? }
- setupWizard?: ChannelPluginSetupWizard
- config: ChannelConfigAdapter<ResolvedAccount>
- configSchema?: ChannelConfigSchema
- setup?: ChannelSetupAdapter
- pairing?: ChannelPairingAdapter
- security?: ChannelSecurityAdapter<ResolvedAccount>
- groups?: ChannelGroupAdapter
- mentions?: ChannelMentionAdapter
- outbound?: ChannelOutboundAdapter
- status?: ChannelStatusAdapter<ResolvedAccount, Probe, Audit>
- gatewayMethods?: string[]
- gateway?: ChannelGatewayAdapter<ResolvedAccount>
- auth?: ChannelAuthAdapter
- approvalCapability?: ChannelApprovalCapability
- elevated?: ChannelElevatedAdapter
- commands?: ChannelCommandAdapter
- lifecycle?: ChannelLifecycleAdapter
- approvals?: ChannelApprovalAdapter
- allowlist?: ChannelAllowlistAdapter
- bindings?: ChannelConfiguredBindingProvider
- conversationBindings?: ChannelConversationBindingSupport
- streaming?: ChannelStreamingAdapter
- threading?: ChannelThreadingAdapter
- messaging?: ChannelMessagingAdapter
- agentPrompt?: ChannelAgentPromptAdapter
- directory?: ChannelDirectoryAdapter
- resolver?: ChannelResolverAdapter
- actions?: ChannelMessageActionAdapter
- heartbeat?: ChannelHeartbeatAdapter
- agentTools?: ChannelAgentToolFactory | ChannelAgentTool[]
```

Also defines: ChannelConfigUiHint, ChannelConfigRuntimeIssue, ChannelConfigRuntimeParseResult, ChannelConfigRuntimeSchema, ChannelConfigSchema

### 15. `/src/plugin-sdk/core.ts` (first 100 lines)
Exports many re-exported types from:
- setup-wizard-types
- setup-wizard
- types.adapters
- types.core

Then defines: `createInlineTextPairingAdapter()` helper function that:
- Takes params: idLabel, message, normalizeAllowEntry?, notify
- Returns ChannelPairingAdapter
- Adapter uses notifyApproval to call the notify function with message

### 16. `/src/plugin-sdk/runtime-store.ts`
Defines `createPluginRuntimeStore<T>(errorMessage: string)` function that:
- Returns object with:
  - `setRuntime(next: T)`: stores runtime
  - `clearRuntime()`: clears runtime
  - `tryGetRuntime()`: returns T | null
  - `getRuntime()`: returns T or throws errorMessage
- Used by both IRC and Nostr plugins

---

## KEY PATTERNS FOR NEW CHANNEL PLUGINS

1. **Package structure**: index.ts entry point, setup-entry.ts, src/types.ts, src/runtime.ts, src/channel.ts
2. **Runtime store pattern**: Use `createPluginRuntimeStore()` helper
3. **Plugin creation**: Use `createChatChannelPlugin()` factory with base/pairing/security/outbound sections
4. **Account resolution**: Scoped config adapters for multi-account support
5. **Direction types**: "direct" (DMs) vs group messages
6. **Pairing**: Text-based with idLabel, normalizeAllowEntry, notify callback
7. **Outbound**: deliveryMode, textChunkLimit, sendText/sendMedia
8. **Gateway**: startAccount with abort signal and cleanup
9. **Status**: Account probing and summary building
10. **Normalization**: Channel-specific ID normalization (IRC: nick/host, Nostr: pubkey)

## SDK TYPES

### 14. `/src/channels/plugins/types.plugin.ts` (first 200 lines)
Defines `ChannelPlugin<ResolvedAccount = any, Probe = unknown, Audit = unknown>` type with these sections:

```
- id: ChannelId
- meta: ChannelMeta
- capabilities: ChannelCapabilities
- defaults?: { queue?: { debounceMs? } }
- reload?: { configPrefixes: string[]; noopPrefixes? }
```

- setup-wizard-types

### 16. `/src/plugin-sdk/runtime-store.ts`

  - `clearRuntime()`: clears runtime
  - `tryGetRuntime()`: returns T | null

- Used by both IRC and Nostr plugins

---

## KEY PATTERNS FOR NEW CHANNEL PLUGINS

1. **Package structure**: index.ts entry point, setup-entry.ts, src/types.ts, src/runtime.ts, src/channel.ts
2. **Runtime store pattern**: Use `createPluginRuntimeStore()` helper

content.text: Now let me check the nostr outbound pattern and the `getChatChannelMeta` function:

content.thinking:
I see that `getChatChannelMeta` only accepts a fixed set of channel IDs: `"telegram" | "whatsapp" | "discord" | "irc" | "googlechat" | "slack" | "signal" | "imessage" | "line"`. Since "pulseboard" isn't in this set, I can't use `getChatChannelMeta` for the new plugin. 

Looking at how IRC uses it via `runtime-api.js`, let me check how channels that aren't in the core list build their meta. Let me check another extension channel like matrix or zalo.


Actually, let me look at how nostr does it instead—since it's also a plugin, it should show me how to construct the meta without relying on the core function.

content.text: I notice `getChatChannelMeta` only accepts core channel IDs. Let me check how extension plugins build their meta:

```ts
  id: "nostr",

  docsLabel: "nostr",

},
```

content.text:
All 7 files are in place. Here's a summary of what was created:

### `extensions/pulseboard/` — New PulseBoard Channel Plugin

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `package.json` | Package metadata with `@openclaw/pulseboard` name, `"module"` type, `openclaw` block with channel id/label/selectionLabel/docsPath/blurb, and workspace dev/peer deps |
| `openclaw.plugin.json` | Plugin manifest with `id: "pulseboard"`, `channels: ["pulseboard"]`, and minimal `configSchema` |
| `src/types.ts` | Three interfaces: `PulseBoardAccount` (boardUrl, apiToken, username), `PulseBoardConfig` (accounts record), `PulseBoardMessage` (id, boardId, authorId, content, timestamp) |
| `src/runtime.ts` | Runtime store via `createPluginRuntimeStore` exporting `getPulseBoardRuntime` and `setPulseBoardRuntime` with error message `"PulseBoard runtime not initialized"` |
| `src/channel.ts` | Channel plugin built with `createChatChannelPlugin()` — inline meta (label, docsPath, blurb), capabilities (direct+group, no media), config/setup/gateway/messaging stubs, outbound with `deliveryMode: "direct"`, `textChunkLimit: 4000`, and a `sendText` that returns channel/to/messageId |
| `index.ts` | Entry point via `defineChannelPluginEntry()` with id/name/description/plugin/setRuntime |
| `setup-entry.ts` | Setup entry via `defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseBoardPlugin)` |

The plugin follows the same patterns as the existing IRC and Nostr extensions — imports only from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*`, uses inline meta (since `getChatChannelMeta` is restricted to core channels), and keeps all adapters as clean stubs ready for real implementation. No existing files were modified.

All 7 files are in place. Here's a summary of what was created:

### `extensions/pulseboard/` — New PulseBoard Channel Plugin

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `package.json` | Package metadata with `@openclaw/pulseboard` name, `"module"` type, `openclaw` block with channel id/label/selectionLabel/docsPath/blurb, and workspace dev/peer deps |
| `openclaw.plugin.json` | Plugin manifest with `id: "pulseboard"`, `channels: ["pulseboard"]`, and minimal `configSchema` |
| `src/types.ts` | Three interfaces: `PulseBoardAccount` (boardUrl, apiToken, username), `PulseBoardConfig` (accounts record), `PulseBoardMessage` (id, boardId, authorId, content, timestamp) |
| `src/runtime.ts` | Runtime store via `createPluginRuntimeStore` exporting `getPulseBoardRuntime` and `setPulseBoardRuntime` with error message `"PulseBoard runtime not initialized"` |
| `src/channel.ts` | Channel plugin built with `createChatChannelPlugin()` — inline meta (label, docsPath, blurb), capabilities (direct+group, no media), config/setup/gateway/messaging stubs, outbound with `deliveryMode: "direct"`, `textChunkLimit: 4000`, and a `sendText` that returns channel/to/messageId |
| `index.ts` | Entry point via `defineChannelPluginEntry()` with id/name/description/plugin/setRuntime |
| `setup-entry.ts` | Setup entry via `defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseBoardPlugin)` |

The plugin follows the same patterns as the existing IRC and Nostr extensions — imports only from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*`, uses inline meta (since `getChatChannelMeta` is restricted to core channels), and keeps all adapters as clean stubs ready for real implementation. No existing files were modified.
